proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `unfit_for_intensive` set have a higher rate of `objective_response` than patients without it.
Patients with `complex_karyotype` set have a lower rate of `objective_response` than patients without it.
Female patients (`sex_female`=1) and male patients differ in `objective_response` rate.
`secondary_aml` is associated with a different `objective_response` rate than de novo AML.

analyses.result_summary: Response 22.1% in unfit vs 16.5% in fit (diff +5.6pp, chi-square p=1.9e-55).
Response 16.8% in complex_karyotype vs 19.1% otherwise (diff -2.3pp, p=3.3e-06).
Response 18.9% in females vs 18.6% in males (diff +0.3pp, p=0.42).
Response 18.8% in secondary AML vs 18.7% in de novo (diff +0.1pp, p=0.85).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Worse `ecog_ps` is associated with a lower rate of `objective_response`.
Older `age_years` is associated with a lower rate of `objective_response`.
Higher `blast_pct_marrow` is associated with a lower rate of `objective_response`.
Higher `wbc_k_per_ul` is associated with a lower rate of `objective_response`.

analyses.result_summary: Response 23.1% (ECOG 0), 17.4% (1), 12.9% (2); chi-square p=1.8e-90. Monotone decrease.
Mean age 67.98 in responders vs 68.00 in non-responders (diff -0.02, t-test p=0.88).
Mean marrow blasts 58.5% in responders vs 60.3% in non-responders (diff -1.8pp, t-test p=2.0e-16).
Mean WBC 19.27 K/uL in responders vs 20.39 in non-responders (diff -1.11, t-test p=1.5e-04).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher `albumin_g_dl` is associated with a higher rate of `objective_response`.
Higher `weight_loss_pct_6mo` is associated with a lower rate of `objective_response`.
Higher `crp_mg_l` is associated with a lower rate of `objective_response`.
Higher `ldh_u_l` is associated with a different rate of `objective_response`.
Higher `nlr` is associated with a lower rate of `objective_response`.

analyses.result_summary: Mean albumin 3.82 in responders vs 3.80 in non-responders (diff +0.029 g/dL, p=2.7e-07).
Mean weight loss 3.44% in responders vs 3.95% in non-responders (diff -0.51pp, p=1.7e-33).
Mean CRP 5.69 mg/L in responders vs 6.17 in non-responders (diff -0.48, p=1.6e-06).
Mean LDH 223.1 U/L in responders vs 224.0 in non-responders (diff -0.87, p=0.35).
Mean NLR 3.50 in responders vs 3.50 in non-responders (diff -0.002, p=0.92).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `npm1_mutation` set have a higher `objective_response` rate than patients without it.
Patients with `tp53_mutation` set have a lower `objective_response` rate than patients without it.
Patients with `flt3_itd` or `flt3_tkd` set have a different `objective_response` rate than patients without those mutations.
Patients with `idh1_mutation` or `idh2_mutation` set have a different `objective_response` rate than patients without them.

analyses.result_summary: Response 23.5% in NPM1+ vs 16.7% in NPM1- (diff +6.7pp, p=5.8e-70). Strong positive prognostic signal.
Response 16.6% in TP53+ vs 19.0% in TP53- (diff -2.4pp, p=3.5e-05). Adverse main effect.

analyses.code: stats.chi2_contingency on flt3_itd and flt3_tkd separately
analyses.result_summary: FLT3-ITD: 18.9% vs 18.7% (p=0.64). FLT3-TKD: 18.0% vs 18.8% (p=0.34). No marginal effect on response.
IDH1: 19.1% vs 18.7% (p=0.60). IDH2: 19.1% vs 18.7% (p=0.47). Neither has a marginal main effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving `treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine` have a higher rate of `objective_response` than patients not receiving it.
Patients receiving `treatment_7plus3` have a different rate of `objective_response` than patients not receiving it.
Patients receiving `treatment_midostaurin` or `treatment_gilteritinib` have a different `objective_response` rate than untreated patients.
Patients receiving `treatment_ivosidenib` or `treatment_enasidenib` have a different `objective_response` rate than untreated patients.

analyses.result_summary: Response 22.0% on ven/aza vs 16.7% off (diff +5.3pp; chi-square p<<0.001). Largest treatment main effect.
Response 18.9% on 7+3 vs 18.7% off (diff +0.2pp; not significant).
Midostaurin: 18.6% vs 18.8% (diff -0.2pp). Gilteritinib: 18.1% vs 18.8% (diff -0.7pp). No meaningful main effect for FLT3 inhibitors.
Ivosidenib: 18.8% vs 18.7%. Enasidenib: 18.6% vs 18.8%. No main effect for IDH inhibitors.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for age, sex, ECOG, mutations, labs and other treatments, `treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine` is independently associated with higher `objective_response`.

After adjustment, `npm1_mutation` is independently associated with higher `objective_response`, while `tp53_mutation` and `complex_karyotype` are independently associated with lower `objective_response`.
After adjustment, `treatment_7plus3`, `treatment_midostaurin`, `treatment_gilteritinib`, `treatment_ivosidenib`, and `treatment_enasidenib` show no main effect on `objective_response`.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ age + sex + ecog + mutations + labs + all 6 treatments', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Multivariable logit (n=50000): ven/aza coef=+0.361 (p<1e-30); 7+3 coef=+0.015 (p=0.52); midostaurin -0.010 (p=0.76); gilteritinib -0.049 (p=0.13); ivosidenib +0.019 (p=0.66); enasidenib -0.010 (p=0.82); npm1 +0.436 (p<1e-30); tp53 -0.188 (p=3.5e-06); complex_karyotype -0.169 (p=6e-07); ecog_ps -0.361 (p<1e-30); unfit +0.373 (p<1e-30); albumin +0.123 (p=1e-07); blast_pct -0.005 (p=2e-17); weight_loss -0.037 (p=3e-31); crp -0.007 (p=6e-06); wbc -0.002 (p=1e-04). Confirms ven/aza is the only treatment with an independent positive main effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The effect of `treatment_midostaurin` on `objective_response` is more positive in patients with `flt3_itd` set than in those without.
The effect of `treatment_gilteritinib` on `objective_response` is more positive in patients with `flt3_itd` set than in those without.
The effect of `treatment_midostaurin` or `treatment_gilteritinib` on `objective_response` is more positive in patients with `flt3_tkd` set than in those without.

analyses.result_summary: FLT3-ITD+: midostaurin 17.6% vs 19.1% (diff -1.5pp). FLT3-ITD-: 18.8% vs 18.7% (diff +0.1pp). Interaction logit coef -0.106 (p=0.19). No evidence midostaurin works better in FLT3-ITD carriers.
FLT3-ITD+: gilteritinib 19.1% vs 18.9% (diff +0.2pp). FLT3-ITD-: 17.9% vs 18.9% (diff -1.0pp). Interaction logit coef +0.079 (p=0.32). No clinically meaningful interaction.
Midostaurin x FLT3-TKD: coef -0.023 (p=0.87). Gilteritinib x FLT3-TKD: coef -0.213 (p=0.16); diff in TKD+ subgroup is -3.5pp (n=418). No supporting interaction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The effect of `treatment_ivosidenib` on `objective_response` is more positive in patients with `idh1_mutation` set than in those without.
The effect of `treatment_enasidenib` on `objective_response` is more positive in patients with `idh2_mutation` set than in those without.

analyses.result_summary: IDH1+: ivosidenib 15.4% vs 19.4% (diff -4.0pp). IDH1-: 19.1% vs 18.7% (diff +0.4pp). Interaction logit coef -0.304 (p=0.077). Direction opposite of hypothesized: ivosidenib does NOT show a benefit in IDH1+ patients (numerically harmful).
IDH2+: enasidenib 19.5% vs 19.1% (diff +0.4pp). IDH2-: 18.5% vs 18.7% (diff -0.2pp). Interaction logit coef +0.039 (p=0.79). No meaningful enrichment in IDH2 carriers.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The effect of `treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine` on `objective_response` is more positive in patients with `npm1_mutation` set than in those without.
The effect of `treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine` on `objective_response` is more positive in patients with `unfit_for_intensive` set than in those without.

analyses.result_summary: NPM1+: ven/aza 34.7% vs 16.0% (diff +18.7pp; n_t=6025/n_c=8989). NPM1-: 16.5% vs 16.9% (diff -0.4pp). Interaction logit coef +1.055 (p=5.5e-102). Massive synergy.
Unfit: ven/aza 30.8% vs 16.3% (diff +14.5pp; n_t=8013/n_c=12043). Fit: 16.0% vs 16.8% (diff -0.8pp). Interaction logit coef +0.883 (p=1.1e-78). Strong synergy.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The effect of `treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine` on `objective_response` is suppressed (no benefit) in patients with `tp53_mutation` set, compared with patients without it.
The effect of `treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine` on `objective_response` is suppressed (no benefit) in patients with `complex_karyotype` set, compared with patients without it.

analyses.result_summary: TP53-: ven/aza 22.6% vs 16.6% (diff +6.0pp). TP53+: 16.4% vs 16.7% (diff -0.4pp). Interaction logit coef -0.409 (p=4.7e-07). TP53 mutation abolishes ven/aza benefit.
CK-: ven/aza 22.7% vs 16.7% (diff +6.1pp). CK+: 17.5% vs 16.3% (diff +1.2pp). Interaction logit coef -0.306 (p=6.0e-06). Complex karyotype abolishes most of the ven/aza benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The effect of `treatment_7plus3` on `objective_response` is more negative in patients with `complex_karyotype` set than in those without.
The effect of `treatment_7plus3` on `objective_response` differs by `tp53_mutation` status.
The effect of `treatment_7plus3` on `objective_response` differs by `secondary_aml` status.

analyses.result_summary: CK+: 7+3 15.5% vs 17.9% (diff -2.3pp). CK-: 19.5% vs 18.8% (diff +0.7pp). Interaction logit coef -0.213 (p=0.0015). 7+3 numerically harmful in complex karyotype.
TP53+: 7+3 16.4% vs 16.7% (diff -0.3pp). TP53-: 19.2% vs 18.8% (diff +0.4pp). Interaction logit coef -0.045 (p=0.57). No meaningful interaction.
Secondary AML: 7+3 18.5% vs 19.1% (diff -0.6pp). De novo: 19.0% vs 18.5% (diff +0.6pp). Interaction logit coef -0.078 (p=0.15). Trend toward harm in secondary AML, not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The benefit of `treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine` is jointly concentrated in patients with both `npm1_mutation` set AND `unfit_for_intensive` set, with little to no benefit when only one of those conditions holds.

analyses.result_summary: Three-way interaction coef +2.126 (p=2e-92). NPM1- & fit: ven/aza 16.2% vs 17.2% (diff -1.0pp). NPM1- & unfit: 16.9% vs 16.4% (diff +0.5pp). NPM1+ & fit: 15.7% vs 16.0% (diff -0.3pp). NPM1+ & unfit: 63.3% vs 16.0% (diff +47.4pp; n=2401/3585). The benefit is essentially confined to NPM1+ AND unfit patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the NPM1+ AND unfit_for_intensive subgroup, the `treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine` benefit on `objective_response` is suppressed when `tp53_mutation` is also set.

analyses.result_summary: In NPM1+/unfit: TP53- patients have ven/aza 69.1% vs 15.7% (diff +53.4pp; n=2125/3224). TP53+ patients have ven/aza 18.8% vs 18.3% (diff +0.5pp; n=276/361). Four-way logistic interaction (ven/aza*npm1*unfit*tp53) coef -2.640 (p=1.3e-13): TP53 mutation completely abolishes the benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within NPM1+ AND unfit_for_intensive AND TP53- patients, the `treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine` benefit on `objective_response` is further suppressed when `complex_karyotype` is set.

analyses.result_summary: Within NPM1+/unfit/TP53-: CK- patients have ven/aza 78.6% vs 15.8% (diff +62.8pp; n=1790/2741). CK+ patients have ven/aza 18.2% vs 14.9% (diff +3.3pp; n=335/483). Complex karyotype completely abolishes the residual ven/aza benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The benefit of `treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine` on `objective_response` is fully captured by the joint subgroup defined by `npm1_mutation`=1 AND `unfit_for_intensive`=1 AND `tp53_mutation`=0 AND `complex_karyotype`=0; outside that subgroup the average treatment effect is approximately zero.

analyses.result_summary: Inside subgroup (n=4531): ven/aza 78.6% (n=1790) vs no ven/aza 15.8% (n=2741); diff +62.8pp. Outside subgroup (n=45469): ven/aza 16.4% (n=18230) vs no ven/aza 16.7% (n=27239); diff -0.3pp. Logistic interaction: ven/aza:subgroup coef +2.993 (z=36.5, p<1e-200).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the NPM1+/unfit/TP53-/CK- subgroup, the `treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine` benefit is further modified by `flt3_itd`, `flt3_tkd`, `idh1_mutation`, `idh2_mutation`, or `secondary_aml`.

analyses.code: Stratify ven/aza effect within full subgroup by additional features
analyses.result_summary: Within NPM1+/unfit/TP53-/CK- (n=4531): ven/aza vs no diff is +53.8pp at FLT3-ITD-, +51.7pp at FLT3-ITD+; +53.4pp at FLT3-TKD-, +53.8pp at TKD+; +53.5pp at IDH1-, +52.2pp at IDH1+; +54.0pp at IDH2-, +47.6pp at IDH2+; +53.3pp at de novo, +53.6pp at secondary AML. No additional feature meaningfully modifies the in-subgroup effect, so the four-predicate definition is complete.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the NPM1+/unfit/TP53-/CK- subgroup, the `treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine` benefit is preserved across ECOG strata.

analyses.code: Stratify ven/aza effect within subgroup by ecog_ps
analyses.result_summary: ECOG 0: ven/aza 74.8% vs 19.6% (diff +55.2pp, n_t=746). ECOG 1: 67.8% vs 14.9% (diff +52.9pp, n_t=1065). ECOG 2: 59.9% vs 9.3% (diff +50.6pp, n_t=314). Effect is large across all ECOG strata; absolute response higher with better ECOG but treatment delta similar.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the NPM1+/unfit/TP53-/CK- subgroup, after adjusting for age, sex, ECOG, mutations, and labs, `treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine` remains independently associated with higher `objective_response`.
Within the NPM1+/unfit/TP53-/CK- subgroup, the other five treatments have no effect on `objective_response`.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ ven_aza + age + sex + ecog + mutations + labs', data=subgroup).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted in-subgroup logit (n=4531): ven/aza coef +3.044 (z=37.9, p<1e-200). ECOG -0.452 (p=2e-14), albumin +0.183 (p=0.018), WBC -0.004 (p=0.008); other covariates not significant. ven/aza independently drives the entire benefit.

Within subgroup, response unchanged by other treatments: midostaurin 40.5% vs 40.6% (diff -0.1pp); gilteritinib 40.6% vs 40.6% (diff 0.0pp); ivosidenib 38.9% vs 40.8% (diff -1.9pp); enasidenib 46.1% vs 40.2% (diff +5.9pp); 7+3 40.4% vs 40.8% (diff -0.4pp). Only ven/aza moves response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Stepwise refinement: each predicate (`npm1_mutation`=1, `unfit_for_intensive`=1, `tp53_mutation`=0, `complex_karyotype`=0) materially increases the in-subgroup ven/aza treatment effect over the next-narrower subgroup.

analyses.code: Compute ven/aza effect for AND subsets of size 1..4 of the four predicates
analyses.result_summary: Marginal (each predicate alone): ven/aza diff = +18.7pp (NPM1+), +14.5pp (unfit), +6.0pp (TP53-), +6.1pp (CK-). Pairs: NPM1+ AND unfit = +47.4pp, NPM1+ AND TP53- = +20.8pp, NPM1+ AND CK- = +21.9pp. Triples: NPM1+ AND unfit AND TP53- = +53.4pp, NPM1+ AND unfit AND CK- = +55.6pp, NPM1+ AND TP53- AND CK- = +24.3pp. Full quadruple (NPM1+ AND unfit AND TP53- AND CK-) = +62.8pp. Each successive predicate enriches the effect; the four-predicate AND is the strongest, monotone subgroup definition.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A systematic single-feature treatment-by-feature interaction screen will identify `treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine` x {`npm1_mutation`, `unfit_for_intensive`, `tp53_mutation`, `complex_karyotype`} as the only highly significant interactions.

analyses.code: for each of 6 treatments x 10 binary features: smf.logit(treatment*feature)
analyses.result_summary: Top interactions by p-value: ven/aza:npm1 (coef +1.055, p=5.5e-102); ven/aza:unfit (+0.883, p=1.1e-78); ven/aza:tp53 (-0.409, p=4.7e-07); ven/aza:complex_karyotype (-0.306, p=6.0e-06); enasidenib:sex_female (+0.314, p=2.2e-04); 7+3:complex_karyotype (-0.213, p=0.0015). Confirms the four ven/aza modifiers dominate the heterogeneity landscape.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The effect of `treatment_enasidenib` on `objective_response` differs by `sex_female`, with female patients benefitting and male patients potentially harmed.

analyses.result_summary: Males: enasidenib 16.5% vs 18.8% (diff -2.3pp; n_t=2191). Females: 21.2% vs 18.7% (diff +2.5pp; n_t=1781). Interaction logit coef +0.314 (p=2.2e-04). Within IDH2+ female patients, response 24.1% on enasidenib vs 20.0% off (diff +4.1pp, n=166). A real but small sex-modified effect; magnitude smaller than ven/aza signal.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative effect of `treatment_7plus3` on `objective_response` in `complex_karyotype` patients is preserved across `tp53_mutation` strata.

analyses.code: Stratify 7+3 effect by complex_karyotype x tp53_mutation
analyses.result_summary: CK+/TP53-: 7+3 15.6% vs 18.0% (diff -2.3pp). CK+/TP53+: 14.8% vs 16.9% (diff -2.1pp). CK-/TP53-: 19.8% vs 19.0% (diff +0.8pp). CK-/TP53+: 16.7% vs 16.7% (diff 0.0pp). The harm of 7+3 in complex karyotype is consistent regardless of TP53 status; not driven by TP53 co-occurrence.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The independent prognostic value of `npm1_mutation` (positive) and `tp53_mutation`/`complex_karyotype` (negative) on `objective_response` is preserved within the no-`treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine` cohort, suggesting these are baseline biology effects rather than purely treatment-mediated.

analyses.code: Within ven/aza==0 subset: stats.chi2_contingency on each binary feature
analyses.result_summary: Ven/aza-untreated (n=29980): NPM1+ 16.0% vs NPM1- 16.9% (diff -0.9pp). TP53+ 16.7% vs 16.7% (no diff). CK+ 16.5% vs 16.7% (diff -0.2pp). Within untreated, the NPM1 'good prognosis' and CK/TP53 'bad prognosis' main effects largely disappear, indicating the marginal mutation-prognosis associations in the full cohort are driven by ven/aza receipt rather than untreated biology. The NPM1+/unfit/TP53-/CK- pattern is therefore a treatment-effect modifier, not a pure prognostic factor.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Final consolidated hypothesis: `treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine` increases `objective_response` only in patients who simultaneously have `npm1_mutation`=1, `unfit_for_intensive`=1, `tp53_mutation`=0, and `complex_karyotype`=0; in this subgroup the absolute response increases from ~16% to ~79% (+63pp). Outside this four-predicate subgroup the average treatment effect is null.

analyses.code: Final subgroup definition; logit with subgroup-by-treatment interaction; in/out comparison
analyses.result_summary: Subgroup-by-ven/aza logit interaction coef +2.993 (z=36.5, p<<1e-200). In-subgroup: 78.6% (1790) vs 15.8% (2741), diff +62.8pp; outside: 16.4% (18230) vs 16.7% (27239), diff -0.3pp. The four predicates are individually necessary: removing any one of them allows non-modifier values to dilute the effect (TP53+: +0.5pp; CK+: +3.3pp; NPM1-: -0.4pp; fit: +5.0pp pooled marginal). Confirmed final subgroup hypothesis.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the NPM1+/unfit/TP53-/CK- subgroup, baseline labs (`albumin_g_dl`, `ecog_ps`, `wbc_k_per_ul`, `blast_pct_marrow`, `weight_loss_pct_6mo`) further refine response prediction but do not modify the treatment-effect direction or eliminate the ven/aza benefit.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ ven_aza + ecog_ps + albumin + wbc + blast + weight_loss', data=subgroup).fit()
analyses.result_summary: In-subgroup adjusted logit retains ven/aza coef +3.04 (p<1e-200) regardless of co-adjustment for clinical labs. ECOG (-0.45, p=2e-14), albumin (+0.18, p=0.02), WBC (-0.004, p=0.008) are independent prognostic factors. None changes the direction or significance of ven/aza. The four-predicate subgroup definition is robust to lab-based confounding.
